title: Command Palette
description: Command Palette provides a searchable action launcher with grouped results, disabled items, keyword matching, empty-result messaging, and keyboard-oriented interaction.
intro: Use `ui-command-palette` for global command search, quick navigation, and action launching in dense workspaces. Good palettes reduce hunting through menus and sidebars by making intent searchable.

featureSections.title: Basic action launcher
featureSections.description: The baseline pattern is a searchable action sheet opened from a global trigger. This is the most common command-palette use case.

featureSections.title: Grouped versus flat commands
featureSections.description: Grouping helps when commands mix navigation, actions, and help entries. Flat lists are fine when the action count is smaller and more uniform.

featureSections.title: Search-heavy, disabled, and configured palettes
featureSections.description: Keyword-heavy palettes and disabled commands are common in power-user flows. Placeholder and empty text also affect how understandable the search experience feels.

featureSections.title: No matching results
featureSections.description: The empty state inside a command palette should stay brief and search-specific, not read like a general empty page.

featureSections.title: Workspace command center
featureSections.description: A command palette usually sits behind a global workspace shortcut and acts as a fast entry point to navigation and high-frequency actions.

accessibility.title: Dialog and result semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-command-palette` renders a modal command surface with a focused search field and a result list. Results behave like selectable options and the component keeps keyboard focus trapped while open.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
The command palette is primarily a keyboard-first surface.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| ArrowUp / ArrowDown | Move the active selection between enabled commands. |
| Enter | Execute the currently selected command. |
| Esc | Close the command palette. |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Move focus within the trapped dialog surface. |

accessibility.title: Labels and discoverability
accessibility.body: Commands should have concise labels, helpful descriptions when needed, and enough keywords to support search. Disabled commands should stay understandable rather than appearing as broken or missing actions.

apiSections.title: CommandPaletteComponent - ui-command-palette
apiSections.lead: Standalone searchable action launcher component. It renders a modal palette with search, grouped results, keyboard navigation, disabled states, and empty-result feedback.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Controls whether the palette is open.
Command dataset to search and render.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits when a command is executed.
{ id, label, description?, icon?, keywords?, action, disabled?, group? }
Command contract used for search, rendering, grouping, and execution.
Grouped result structure derived internally from the item list.

paragraphs: Keep command labels short and scannable. Descriptions and keywords should help with discovery, not compensate for unclear labels.
Grouping is useful when the palette mixes navigation and actions. If the list is already short and uniform, a flat result set is often easier to scan.
blocks.title: Notes
